what_it_does: Defines a content node that exposes the right-hand-man presence mode and a voice toggle as static enum configuration, and returns the selected mode string.
observations: The module follows a node substrate pattern with VERSION, KIND, PORTS_IN, PORTS_OUT, CONFIG, and a run function.
PORTS_IN is empty and PORTS_OUT has a single Text port named 'mode'.
CONFIG contains a 'mode' enum with eight options and a default of 'listening'.
CONFIG also contains a 'voice_enabled' enum with options 'on'/'off' and default 'on'.
Comments explain that 'voice_enabled' is global on the single node, not literally per-mode, and that an enum is used because the inspector has no bool widget.
run() ignores inputs and returns config.get('mode', 'listening').
standouts: The file treats the operator's presence preference as a first-class node in the canvas/composition system.
The same create_node/set_config verbs used to build graphs are used to configure system behavior.
A boolean concept (voice on/off) is deliberately modeled as an enum to fit existing inspector widgets.
conventions: Uses the standard node module layout (VERSION, KIND, PORTS_IN, PORTS_OUT, CONFIG, run).
Config is nested under keys like 'mode' and 'voice_enabled' for inspector rendering.
Uses extensive inline comments to document design rationale and migration notes.
concerns: run() takes inputs but does not use them.
The 'voice_enabled' field is global despite the surrounding per-mode language, which the file itself flags as a decision.
A boolean toggle is represented as an enum because the canvas lacks a bool widget, which may confuse semantics.
notes: A thin content node that turns the RHM presence mode into an inspector-editable graph parameter, plus a global voice toggle shoehorned into the existing enum widget.
questions: How is the selected mode consumed downstream?
How does suite.py keep these eight options synchronized with its own MODES list?
What consumes the 'voice_enabled' config and where is the voice gate implemented?
Why is PORTS_IN empty if this node conceptually receives no runtime input?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to make the RHM mode a node whose parameter is edited like any graph config, and the code does exactly that by exposing enum config and returning the selected mode.
intention_signals: Encode the operator's presence preference as a composable node so the same graph-editing verbs that build the operator's work also configure the assistant's behavior, while adding a voice toggle without inventing new UI widgets.
novelty: It folds a system-level setting (RHM presence mode) into the same content-node substrate used for user-facing graph composition, making system configuration indistinguishable from graph editing.
contribution: Provides a reusable node substrate that exposes a presence-mode dial and a voice toggle as inspectable, editable enum config, keeping the system's own behavior configurable through the same node-composition interface as any other graph element.
summary_for_embedding: A content node that exposes the right-hand-man presence mode and a global voice toggle as inspector-editable enum config and returns the selected mode string.
intention_for_embedding: To represent the operator's presence preference as a first-class node in the canvas/composition system so switching modes uses the same create_node/set_config verbs as any graph edit.

symbols.does: Returns the configured RHM mode string, defaulting to 'listening'.
symbols.description: The run function is the runtime output of the presence-mode node. It accepts inputs and config but only reads config['mode'], falling back to 'listening' if the key is absent, and emits the selected mode on the 'mode' output port.
